title: PI Panic
subtitle: Distributed alert and monitoring system for networked devices
description: A distributed alert system designed to monitor device states and trigger escalations based on severity and priority.
Supports real-time communication between nodes and integrates with both UI displays and external systems.
features: Real-time alert and panic escalation system
GUI and optional web interface support
Expandable API for external trigger integration
IO integration for lights, sensors, and external hardware
statusDetails: API layer in development for external triggers
IO integration in progress for hardware interaction
System being refined for usability and expansion

progressNotes: Initial concept and system design (July 2025)
Designed distributed alert system structure.
Defined panic states, priorities, and message format.
Planned communication model between nodes and master system.
Core communication and backend development (July 2025 to Aug 2025)
Implemented MQTT-based communication system.
Built message handling for panic states and updates.
Established continuous listener and transmit loops.
Alert handling and escalation logic (Aug 2025 to Sep 2025)
Developed priority-based alert system.
Implemented escalation rules for panic conditions.
Standardized event data format across system.
GUI system and display integration (Sep 2025 to Oct 2025)
Built fullscreen GUI for alert display.
Added flashing states and visual indicators.
Integrated dynamic updates from backend events.
System expansion and modular structure (Oct 2025 to Dec 2025)
Refactored system into modular components.
Added support for optional modules and hooks.
Improved reliability and handling of multiple nodes.
Testing, debugging, and stability improvements (Dec 2025 to Jan 2026)
Tested system across multiple nodes and conditions.
Resolved communication edge cases and failures.
Improved reconnection and error handling behavior.
Completion and post-build improvements (Jan 2026 to Feb 2026)
Finalized core system functionality.
Prepared system for real-world usage.
Began development of external API for triggering alerts.
Started integration of IO for lights and sensors.
